title: Durable Objects in Dynamic Workers: Give each AI-generated app its own database
summary: We’re introducing Durable Object Facets, allowing Dynamic Workers to instantiate Durable Objects with their own isolated SQLite databases. This enables developers to build platforms that run persistent, stateful code generated on-the-fly.

title: Agents have their own computers with Sandboxes GA
summary: Cloudflare Sandboxes give AI agents a persistent, isolated environment: a real computer with a shell, a filesystem, and background processes that starts on demand and picks up exactly where it left off.

title: Dynamic, identity-aware, and secure Sandbox auth
summary: Outbound Workers for Sandboxes provide a programmable, zero-trust egress proxy for AI agents. This allows developers to inject credentials and enforce dynamic security policies without exposing sensitive tokens to untrusted code.

title: Unlocking next generation audio production with MPEG-H on AWS
summary: In this post, learn the implementation of cloud-based NGA production workflows using MPEG-H Audio on AWS. By combining Jünger Audio's audio processing expertise, MainConcept's encoding capabilities, Techex's stream processing platform, and …

title: Welcome to Agents Week
summary: Cloudflare's mission has always been to help build a better Internet. Sometimes that means building for the Internet as it exists. Sometimes it means building for the Internet as it's about to become. This week, we're kicking off Agents Wee…

title: 500 Tbps of capacity: 16 years of scaling our global network
summary: Cloudflare’s global network has officially crossed 500 Tbps of external capacity, enough to route more than 20% of the web and absorb the largest DDoS attacks ever recorded.

title: From bytecode to bytes: automated magic packet generation
summary: By applying symbolic execution and the Z3 theorem prover to BPF bytecode, we’ve automated the generation of malware trigger packets, cutting analysis time from hours to seconds.

title: Cloudflare targets 2029 for full post-quantum security
summary: Recent advances in quantum hardware and software have accelerated the timeline on which quantum attack might happen. Cloudflare is responding by moving our target for full post-quantum security to 2029.

title: How we built Organizations to help enterprises manage Cloudflare at scale
summary: Cloudflare Organizations is now in public beta, introducing a new management layer for enterprise customers with multiple accounts. Learn how we consolidated our authorization systems to enable org-wide management.

title: Our ongoing commitment to privacy for the 1.1.1.1 public DNS resolver
summary: Eight years ago, we launched 1.1.1.1 to build a faster, more private Internet. Today, we’re sharing the results of our latest independent examination. The result: our privacy protections are working exactly as promised.

title: Introducing EmDash — the spiritual successor to WordPress that solves plugin security
summary: Today we are launching the beta of EmDash, a full-stack serverless JavaScript CMS built on Astro 6.0. It combines the features of a traditional CMS with modern security, running plugins in sandboxed Worker isolates.

title: Introducing Programmable Flow Protection: custom DDoS mitigation logic for Magic Transit customers
summary: Magic Transit customers can now program their own DDoS mitigation logic and deploy it across Cloudflare’s global network. This enables precise, stateful mitigation for custom and proprietary UDP protocols.

title: Cloudflare Client-Side Security: smarter detection, now open to everyone

title: How we use Abstract Syntax Trees (ASTs) to turn Workflows code into visual diagrams
summary: Workflows are now visualized via step diagrams in the dashboard. Here’s how we translate your TypeScript code into a visual representation of the workflow.

title: A one-line Kubernetes fix that saved 600 hours a year
summary: When we investigated why our Atlantis instance took 30 minutes to restart, we discovered a bottleneck in how Kubernetes handles volume permissions. By adjusting the fsGroupChangePolicy, we reduced restart times to 30 seconds.

title: Sandboxing AI agents, 100x faster
summary: We’re introducing Dynamic Workers, which allow you to execute AI-generated code in secure, lightweight isolates. This approach is 100 times faster than traditional containers, enabling millisecond startup times for AI agent sandboxing.

title: Introducing Custom Regions for precision data control
summary: We are expanding Regional Services with new pre-defined regions and the launch of Custom Regions. Customers can now define precise geographical boundaries for data processing, tailored to meet their compliance and performance needs.
